title: Consent Document Validation Gatekeeping
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: consent document validation gatekeeping.
assumptions: A single ClinicalReviewer is exclusively assigned to a ConsentCase while the assignment predicate is active.
DocumentSource and supporting documents are uniquely identifiable and can be bound/unbound without duplication.
GovernanceChannel selections (ethics, risk) represent mutually available escalation routes; a case may require one or both based on validation results.
ApprovalAuthority instances represent authoritative sign-off entities and are required for cases flagged by the validation method as high-complexity.
risks: Delay risk when required specialist or governance resources are unavailable, causing prolonged case open time.
Privacy risk if DocumentSource or SupportingDocument resources are mishandled during binding/unbinding operations.
Misassignment risk if ClinicalReviewer eligibility relations are incorrect, resulting in reviewers lacking necessary competency.
Incomplete audit trail risk if resource release or finalization actions fail to execute, leaving cases in indeterminate states.
Governance nonconformance risk if ApprovalAuthority approvals are bypassed or inadequately recorded.
